explanation: This property states that globally, if the autopilot is currently in the PULLUP state and both the system is supported and sensor data is good, then in the very next time step, the autopilot must transition to the TRANSITION state and simultaneously exit the PULLUP state. This is the most precise and common interpretation for a triggered, immediate state transition.

This property states that globally, if the autopilot is currently in the PULLUP state and both the system is supported and sensor data is good, then in the very next time step, the autopilot must transition to the TRANSITION state. This assumes states are mutually exclusive, so entering TRANSITION implicitly means leaving PULLUP.

This property states that globally, if the autopilot is currently in the PULLUP state and both the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually, the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state and simultaneously exit the PULLUP state. This allows for some delay between the conditions being met and the state change.

This property states that globally, if the autopilot is currently in the PULLUP state and both the system is supported and sensor data is good, then eventually, the autopilot will enter the TRANSITION state. This allows for delays and implicitly assumes states are mutually exclusive.

This property implies that if the system is in the PULLUP state, it will remain in PULLUP until it transitions to the TRANSITION state, and this transition happens at a time when both `supported` and `good` are true. This captures 'when' as a condition for the state change itself.

Similar to the above, this property states that if the system is in PULLUP, it remains in PULLUP until it transitions to TRANSITION (explicitly exiting PULLUP), and this event must occur precisely when the system is supported and sensor data is good.

If the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION and the `supported` and `good` conditions still hold. This suggests the conditions persist into the new state.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good) )

explanation: If the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then eventually it transitions to TRANSITION, and at that point, the `supported` and `good` conditions are still true.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> F (state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good) )

explanation: If the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next step, it transitions to TRANSITION, explicitly exits PULLUP, and the `supported` and `good` conditions still hold.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & ~state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) )

explanation: If the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then eventually it transitions to TRANSITION, explicitly exits PULLUP, and at that point, the `supported` and `good` conditions are still true.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> F (state_is_TRANSITION & ~state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) )

explanation: This property states that any immediate transition from PULLUP to TRANSITION (where PULLUP is exited) can only occur if the system was previously in PULLUP and the `supported` and `good` conditions were met. This provides a necessary condition for the transition.
If the autopilot is currently in PULLUP and `supported` and `good` will be true in the next step, then it must transition to TRANSITION and exit PULLUP in that same next step.
If the autopilot is currently in PULLUP and `supported` and `good` will be true in the next step, then it must transition to TRANSITION in that same next step.
If the autopilot is in PULLUP and `supported` and `good` are true now and in the next step, then it must immediately transition to TRANSITION and exit PULLUP.
If the autopilot is in PULLUP and `supported` and `good` are true now and in the next step, then it must immediately transition to TRANSITION.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION, and PULLUP must be false in the step after that.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION and remains in TRANSITION in the subsequent step.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION and the `supported` and `good` conditions persist into the state after the transition.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then eventually, in some future step, the system will transition to TRANSITION in the next immediate step.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then eventually it will transition to TRANSITION and remain in TRANSITION for at least two steps (current and next).
If the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then from the very next state onwards, it will eventually transition to TRANSITION and exit PULLUP.
If the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then from the very next state onwards, it will eventually enter the TRANSITION state.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it will be in TRANSITION and remain in TRANSITION for at least two additional steps.

This property states that if the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it must be in TRANSITION either now or in the next state. However, 'change states' suggests not already being in TRANSITION.

This property states that if the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it must eventually transition to TRANSITION and currently not be in PULLUP. The 'currently' aspect for !state_is_PULLUP is less common for an 'F' consequent.
Globally, if the system is in PULLUP, it must remain in PULLUP until it transitions to TRANSITION (while `supported` and `good` are true) and remains in TRANSITION in the subsequent step.
output_LTL: G ( state_is_PULLUP U (state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good & X state_is_TRANSITION) )

explanation: Globally, if in PULLUP, it must remain in PULLUP until it transitions to TRANSITION (while `supported` and `good` are true) and eventually remains in TRANSITION.
output_LTL: G ( state_is_PULLUP U (state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good & F state_is_TRANSITION) )

explanation: If the autopilot is in PULLUP, then if it transitions to TRANSITION in the next step, it must be because `supported` and `good` were true. This is a necessary condition based on the *result* of the transition.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it transitions to TRANSITION and never returns to PULLUP. This implies a permanent state change.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it will remain in PULLUP until it permanently enters the TRANSITION state.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> (state_is_PULLUP U G state_is_TRANSITION) )

explanation: If eventually the trigger conditions are met while in PULLUP, then eventually there will be a transition from PULLUP to TRANSITION. This is a weaker global condition.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next step, it transitions to TRANSITION and eventually permanently exits PULLUP.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & F G ~state_is_PULLUP) )

explanation: If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next step, it transitions to TRANSITION and perpetually eventually returns to TRANSITION. This implies oscillation or recurring transitions.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & G F state_is_TRANSITION) )

explanation: If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it transitions to TRANSITION and these conditions and the TRANSITION state persist for the next step.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> (state_is_PULLUP U (state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good & X (state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good))) )

explanation: If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then eventually it stabilizes in a state where it is perpetually in TRANSITION and out of PULLUP.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it must be in TRANSITION now and in the next step. This contradicts 'change states' implying a transition.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it will be in TRANSITION either now or in the next step. This also contradicts 'change states' if it's already in TRANSITION.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION and the `supported` and `good` conditions persist for at least two steps.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & G[0,2] (supported & good)) )

explanation: If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION and eventually `supported` and `good` will remain true forever.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & F G (supported & good)) )

explanation: If in PULLUP, it must remain in PULLUP until it transitions to TRANSITION and the `supported` and `good` conditions become true in the next step.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> (state_is_PULLUP U (state_is_TRANSITION & X (supported & good))) )

explanation: If in PULLUP, then `supported` and `good` must hold continuously until it transitions to TRANSITION. This implies a stronger continuous requirement for the conditions.
If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it is no longer in PULLUP, and eventually it will be in TRANSITION. This allows for a delay in reaching TRANSITION while out of PULLUP.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (~state_is_PULLUP & F state_is_TRANSITION) )

explanation: If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION, and eventually (from the state after next) it will not be in PULLUP anymore.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & X F ~state_is_PULLUP) )

explanation: If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then eventually it will transition to TRANSITION, and from the next step after that, it will eventually be in TRANSITION again. This implies a return to the TRANSITION state.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> F (state_is_TRANSITION & X F state_is_TRANSITION) )

explanation: If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION, and eventually it will permanently stay in TRANSITION.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & X F G state_is_TRANSITION) )

explanation: If in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it transitions to TRANSITION, remains in TRANSITION for at least one additional step, and is out of PULLUP for at least one additional step.
output_LTL: G ( (state_is_PULLUP & supported & good) -> X (state_is_TRANSITION & G[0,1] ~state_is_PULLUP & G[0,1] state_is_TRANSITION) )

explanation: If currently in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it must remain in PULLUP until it transitions to TRANSITION and exits PULLUP in the *next* state, and at that next state, the `supported` and `good` conditions are still true.
If the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then in the next state, it will be in TRANSITION and is never again in PULLUP.
If the autopilot is in PULLUP and conditions are met, then it must eventually transition to TRANSITION, and subsequently, it will always remain outside the PULLUP state.

This property states that the system will remain in any state where it is not in TRANSITION until it enters the PULLUP state, and at that exact moment, the system is supported and sensor data is good. This inverts the sequence of events and states.
